aup_force_ssl_inspection.description: For First Time AUP Behavior, Force SSL Inspection. When set, Zscaler will force SSL Inspection in order to enforce AUP for HTTPS traffic.

aup_timeout_in_days.description: Custom AUP Frequency. Refresh time (in days) to re-validate the AUP.

caution_enabled.description: Enable Caution. When set to true, a caution notifcation is enabled for the location.

ipv6_enabled.description: If set to true, IPv6 is enabled for the location and IPv6 traffic from the location can be forwarded to the Zscaler service to enforce security policies.

kerberos_auth.description: Enable Kerberos Authentication at the location

name.description: Location Name.

ofw_enabled.description: Enable Firewall. When set to true, Firewall is enabled for the location.

other6_sub_location.description: If set to true, indicates that this is a default sub-location created by the Zscaler service to accommodate IPv6 addresses that are not part of any user-defined sub-locations. other_sub_location.description: If set to true, indicates that this is a default sub-location created by the Zscaler service to accommodate IPv4 addresses that are not part of any user-defined sub-locations. The default sub-location is created with the name Other and it can be renamed, if required.

parent_id.description: Parent Location ID. If this ID does not exist or is 0, it is implied that it is a parent location. Otherwise, it is a sub-location whose parent has this ID. x-applicableTo: SUB

state.description: IP ports that are associated with the location.

sub_loc_scope.description: Defines a scope for the sublocation from the available types to segregate workload traffic from a single sublocation to apply different Cloud Connector and ZIA security policies.

surrogate_ip.description: Enable Surrogate IP. When set to true, users are mapped to internal device IP addresses.

surrogate_ip_enforced_for_known_browsers.description: Enforce Surrogate IP for Known Browsers. When set to true, IP Surrogate is enforced for all known browsers.

surrogate_refresh_time_in_minutes.description: Refresh Time for re-validation of Surrogacy. The surrogate refresh time (in minutes) to re-validate the IP surrogates.

surrogate_refresh_time_unit.description: Display Refresh Time Unit. The time unit to display for refresh time for re-validation of surrogacy.

tz.description: Timezone of the location. If not specified, it defaults to GMT.

up_bandwidth.description: Upload bandwidth in bytes. The value 0 implies no Bandwidth Control enforcement.

xff_forward_enabled.description: Enable XFF Forwarding. When set to true, traffic is passed to Zscaler Cloud via the X-Forwarded-For (XFF) header.

zapp_ssl_scan_enabled.description: Enable Zscaler App SSL Setting. When set to true, the Zscaler App SSL Scan Setting will take effect, irrespective of the SSL policy that is configured for the location.

admin_audit_logs.description: Filters applications based on their support for logging and tracking all administrative activities to identify potential security threats.

certifications.description: List of certifications to be included or excluded for the profile

data_breach.description: Filters applications based on their history of reported data breaches in the last three years.

data_encryption_in_transit.description: Filters applications based on their support for encrypting data in transit

dns_caa_policy.description: Filters applications based on their implementation of the DNS Certification Authority Authorization (CAA) policy that helps prevent unauthorized SSL certificates.

domain_based_message_auth.description: Filters applications based on their support for Domain-Based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance (DMARC), which helps prevent email spoofing and phishing attacks.

domain_keys_identified_mail.description: Filters applications based on their support for DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) authentication, which helps prevent email tampering.

evasive.description: Filters applications based on their support for anonymous access without requiring user authentication that can increase the risk of malicious activity

exclude_certificates.description: Indicates if the certificates are included or not

file_sharing.description: Filters applications based on their support for file sharing features that can increase the risk of data exfiltration

http_security_headers.description: Filters applications based on their implementation of all security headers (X-XSS-Protection, X-Frame-Options, Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy, and X-Content-Type-Options) to protect against common web attacks. This field is not applicable to the Lite API.

id.description: Unique identifier for the risk profile

malware_scanning_for_content.description: Filters applications based on their support for content malware scanning

mfa_support.description: Filters applications based on their support for multi-factor authentication to enhance user account security.

password_strength.description: Filters applications based on their password strength requirements under Hosting Info.

poor_items_of_service.description: Filters applications based on the presence of questionable terms and conditions in their legal agreements, such as sharing customer data with third-party applications.

profile_id.description: Unique identifier for the risk profile.

profile_type.description: Risk profile type. The default profile type is CLOUD_APPLICATIONS

remote_screen_sharing.description: Filters applications based on their support for remote access screen sharing, which can increase the risk of data exfiltration if not properly secured.

risk_index.description: The risk index number of the cloud applications. It represents the risk score assigned to each cloud application based on the risk attribute values.

sender_policy_framework.description: Filters applications based on their support for sender policy framework (SPF) authentication, which helps prevent email spoofing.

source_ip_restrictions.description: Filters applications based on their ability to restrict access to specific IP addresses, reducing the attack surface.

ssl_cert_key_size.description: Filters applications based on the key size of their SSL certificates

ssl_cert_validity.description: Filters applications based on the validity period of their SSL certificates.

ssl_pinned.description: Filters applications based on their use of pinned SSL certificates, making it difficult for attackers to decrypt and validate traffic.

support_for_waf.description: Filters applications based on their support for web application firewalls (WAFs) to protect against common web attacks.

vulnerability.description: Filters applications based on their published CVE vulnerabilities

vulnerability_disclosure.description: Filters applications based on their policy for disclosing known vulnerabilities, allowing ethical hackers to report potential security threats

vulnerable_to_heart_bleed.description: Filters applications based on their vulnerability to the Heartbleed attack

vulnerable_to_log_jam.description: Filters applications based on their vulnerability to the Logjam attack

vulnerable_to_poodle.description: Filters applications based on their vulnerability to the POODLE attack

weak_cipher_support.description: Filters applications based on their support for weak ciphers with key sizes less than 128 bits that can compromise SSL connections.

block.description: List of custom tags to be included or excluded for the profile.

file_hashes_to_be_blocked.description: A custom list of unique MD5 file hashes that must be blocked by Sandbox. A maximum of 10000 MD5 file hashes can be blocked

url.description: The URL or hash identifier for the MD5 entry.

url_comment.description: Optional comment describing the URL or hash entry.

block.description: A custom list of MD5 hash values with metadata for sandbox blocking. Each entry includes a URL, optional comment, and type (e.g. MALWARE).
